pages.title: Picker Wheel - Spin the Wheel to Decide a Random Choice
pages.text:
Picker Wheel - Spin the Wheel to Decide a Random Choice Switch Wheel File Settings Share Tools Picker Wheel Help you to make a random decision 
    This is a random wheel spinner that can decide a choice for you. The choices you inserted will be displayed in this wheel. You can either insert the choices by adding individually or adding as list. After you spin the wheel, the Picker Wheel decides a random result. There are 3 modes available from this randomizer wheel which are normal, elimination and accumulation mode. Each mode has its specify usage such as in the classroom, games or contest. Let use this spinning wheel to help you make a decision.
 Main INPUTS Select Weight Label Input Color Tool Settings (Click to Expand) Version  99   (Jan 05) Quick Tool Links:  Team Picker Wheel ,  Yes No Picker Wheel ,  Number Picker Wheel ,  Letter Picker Wheel ,  Country Picker Wheel ,  State Picker Wheel ,  Color Picker Wheel ,  Image Picker Wheel ,  Date Picker Wheel ,  Instagram Comment Picker Wheel ,  MLB Picker Wheel ,  NBA Picker Wheel ,  NFL Picker Wheel 
 Picker Wheel - Spin the Wheel to Decide a Random Choice 
 
 Table of Contents Show/Hide What Is Picker Wheel? How to Use This Spinner? Wheel Customization? File Storage - How to Save/Open/Delete File? Share Wheel / Collect User Results Create New Wheel and Switch Wheel Modify Title Section Enable Weight, Label, or Color View All Results or Scores Share Results Full Screen View Types of Action Modes Premium Personalized Plan Picker Wheel Family Save as an App in Your Device 
 1. What Is Picker Wheel? 
 A stylish wheel spinner with various functions & customization. Just enter inputs, spin the wheel and get your random result. 
 In some situations, our brain just can't make a decision, so why not let the wheel spinner making the small decision. 
 By just inserting your inputs and spin wheel, you will immediately get a random result. 
 The wheel spinner will give you the fairest result by using the advanced algorithm behind it. 
 People often use the tool for raffles, teachings, remote events, and many other places. 
 Sections below explain the deep-dive of the Picker Wheel features. 
 
 2. How to Use This Spinner? 
 Picker Wheel is a fast and easy random picker in only 3 main steps. Insert inputs, spin the wheel, and get the result. It has many features which make decision-solving fun. 
 Picker Wheel is very easy to use. Below are the few steps for using the spinner to pick a random choice. 
 2.1. Insert Inputs 
 Method 1: Main Mode 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Insert text or image inputs. You can mix both of them. 
 
 
 Insert the text input one by one by clicking the + button or return key from your device. You can also paste multiple lines to add multiple inputs at once. 
 
 
 Insert image inputs by clicking the image input button to add one or multiple images at a time.
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Insert optional inputs such as weight, label, or color for each text or image input by enabling them with the checkbox.
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Lists of inputs are displayed. You can edit, duplicate, hide, or delete each entry.
You can add both text and image in each entry. All changes are instantly reflected on the wheel spinner. 
 
 
 By default, Duplicate, Hide/Show and Delete buttons are shown.
 
 
 
 
 
 
 When the label is enabled, the buttons switch to Hide/Show and Edit.
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Clicking Edit button opens a pop-up, which provides another way to modify the inputs besides editing them directly in the list.
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 As an alternative way to edit, you can click and drag the dot at the bottom right of the textbox to highlight multiple rows or columns. On desktop, use Shift + click to quickly select multiple cells. Hold Ctrl (Windows) or ⌘ Command (Mac) and click to add multiple individual selections. 
 
 
 Then, right-click (desktop) or tap (mobile) on the highlighted cells to access Cut, Copy, Paste, or Clear options. You can also choose Duplicate, Hide/Show, or Delete icons to modify the entire input row.
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Or, if you don’t want to use the context menu, highlight the inputs you want to modify and click the Select button. This enters Select mode, where you can select or deselect rows one-by-one using the radio buttons next to each input for modification.
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 In Select mode, for faster multi-selection, you can highlight multiple rows and right-click (desktop) or tap (mobile) to select or deselect all highlighted rows at once. Once selected, you can edit Weight and Color (if enabled), or apply Duplicate, Hide/Show, or Delete to all selected inputs.
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Method 2: Text Mode 
 
 
 
 
 In text mode, you can add inputs in two ways: import text inputs via CSV, or manually insert text and image inputs with optional weights. 
 
 
 Import by CSV: You can upload a .csv file containing the desired inputs. Refer to the sample in the CSV button for examples with and without weight.
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Insert text or image inputs. You can mix both of them. 
 
 
 Insert the text input by typing directly or by copying and pasting it from elsewhere.
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Insert one or more image inputs by clicking the “Add Image(s)” button.
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Include weight in the formula 
 
 
 Add an optional weight for each text or image input by enabling the checkbox.
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Start each line of your inputs with a weight, followed by a comma (,). E.g. 2,First Input 
 
 
 When no weight is detected, a weight of 1 is assigned. 
 
 
 
 
 Lists of inputs are displayed. You can hide an entry by adding ~ at the start and end of the input. You can include both text and images in each entry. All changes are instantly reflected on the wheel spinner. The images below show examples of inputs without weight and with weight. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 If you wish to insert optional inputs such as label or color for each text or image input, please switch to  Main Mode . 
 
 
 
 2.2. Spin Wheel & Get Result 
 
 
 Click the Spin button from the random wheel or press "Ctrl + Enter" keys to start spinning wheel.
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Picker Wheel announces the choice selected where its pointer points at the end after the wheel spin. 
 
 
 Choose one of the action modes for the choice selected. Or press keyboard Tab / Enter / Esc to switch / select / close actions.
 
 
 
 
 
 
 2.3. Input Actions 
 
 
 There are four function buttons in the Main Mode Inputs section (see image 1), and another four in the Text Mode Inputs section (see image 2).
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Select button - To select multiple inputs to modify their weight or color together, or to duplicate, enable/disable, or delete them. 
 
 
 Shuffle button - Shuffle all the entries before going to spin the wheel. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Title button - To modify tool title, description, and popup result title(s). Please refer  Modify Title Section  for details. 
 
 
 More button - Many other functions such as searching the inputs (Main Mode only), enabling mystery wheel or mystery result, sort inputs, and delete empty inputs.
 
 
 
 
 
 Search Inputs (search inputs, labels, #hex, or #nocolor values from the input list) 
 Enable Mystery Wheel (replace inputs on wheels with “?”) 
 Enable Mystery Result (result hidden until you press keyboard Enter or click to reveal) 
 A to Z Sort (sort inputs alphabetically) 
 Manual Sort (manually reorder entries before spinning the wheel) 
 Delete Empty Inputs (enabled only if empty input rows exist; shows empty row count, click to delete) 
 
 
 
 Weight button – Enable the checkbox to include weights in the formula in Text Mode. 
 
 
 Image button – Insert one or more image inputs at the end of the inputs in Text Mode. 
 
 
 
 
 There are two toggle buttons near the Inputs section.
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Show/Hide Inputs - Toggle the input list on or off. 
 
 
 Show/Hide All Results - Toggle the all results list on or off. 
 
 
 Only one list can be displayed at a time. Hiding both lists allows everyone to focus fully on the wheel. 
 
 
 Those are the basic simple...
